description_long: This is a small, single-engine seaplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The aircraft features prominent floats beneath each wing for water landings, and its tail section is conventional with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engine, with a single seat visible. The exterior is primarily light gray with Polish Air Force insignia on the tail and fuselage, and a yellow accent on the top of the tail fin. The landing gear includes a small nose wheel and main gear integrated into the floats, suitable for amphibious operations.
description: A small, single-engine seaplane with floats, open cockpit, and Polish Air Force markings in light gray and yellow.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open cockpit, single float under the wing, and Polish insignia on the tail and fuselage.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin floats, tail structure, and the full wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features as view 0, showing the cockpit, floats, and insignia.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, propeller hub, cockpit opening, and the floats extending from the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Single piston engine mounted at the nose.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two main floats under the wings and a small nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine in a forward-mid fuselage position.
